notes: The response is predominantly solution-framed (feature list, delivery timeline, next steps) rather than problem-framed; it critically fails to surface the FMA 30-day SEN constraint as a genuine blocker that invalidates the March 31 mandate, treats the board deadline as immovable rather than challenging it, names no distinct personas with role/timing/cost, lists no out-of-scope items with reasons, and frames the hardship fee waiver and SEN timeline as minor edge cases rather than material uncertainties with risk-if-wrong consequences.
